options.title: microvm.alioth.package
options.description: The alioth package to use.

options.title: microvm.balloon
options.description:
Whether to enable ballooning.

By "inflating" or increasing the balloon the host can reduce the VMs
memory amount and reclaim it for itself.
When "deflating" or decreasing the balloon the host can give the memory
back to the VM.

virtio-mem is recommended over ballooning if supported by the hypervisor.

options.title: microvm.binScripts
options.type: attribute set of strings concatenated with "\n"
options.description: Script snippets that end up in the runner package's bin/ directory

options.title: microvm.bootDisk
options.description:
Generated.

Required for Hypervisors that do not support direct
kernel+initrd loading.

options.title: microvm.cloud-hypervisor.extraArgs
options.description: Extra arguments to pass to cloud-hypervisor.

options.title: microvm.cloud-hypervisor.package
options.description: The cloud-hypervisor package to use.

options.title: microvm.cloud-hypervisor.platformOEMStrings
options.description:
Extra arguments to pass to cloud-hypervisor's --platform oem_strings=[] argument.

All the oem strings will be concatenated with a comma (,) and wrapped in oem_string=[].

Do not include oem_string= or the [] brackets in the value.

The resulting string will be combined with any --platform options in
`config.microvm.cloud-hypervisor.extraArgs` and passed as a single
--platform option to cloud-hypervisor

options.title: microvm.cpu
options.description:
What CPU to emulate, if any. If different from the host
architecture, it will have a serious performance hit.

::: {.note}
Only supported with qemu.
:::

options.title: microvm.credentialFiles
options.description: Key-value pairs of credential files that will be loaded into the vm using systemd's io.systemd.credential feature.

options.title: microvm.crosvm.extraArgs
options.description: Extra arguments to pass to crosvm.

options.title: microvm.crosvm.package
options.description: The crosvm package to use.

options.title: microvm.crosvm.pivotRoot
options.description: A Hypervisor's sandbox directory

options.title: microvm.declaredRunner
options.description: Generated Hypervisor declared by `config.microvm.hypervisor`

options.title: microvm.deflateOnOOM
options.description: Whether to enable automatic balloon deflation on out-of-memory.

options.title: microvm.deploy.installOnHost
options.description:
Use this script to deploy the working state of your local
Flake on a target host that imports
`microvm.nixosModules.host`:

```
nix run .#nixosConfigurations.nixos.config.microvm.deploy.installOnHost root@example.com
ssh root@example.com systemctl restart microvm@nixos
```

- Evaluate this MicroVM to a derivation
- Copy the derivation to the target host
- Build the MicroVM runner on the target host
- Install/update the MicroVM on the target host

Can be followed by either:
- `systemctl restart microvm@nixos.service` on the
  target host, or
- `config.microvm.deploy.sshSwitch`

options.title: microvm.deploy.rebuild
options.description:
`config.microvm.deploy.installOnHost` and `.sshSwitch` in one
script. Akin to what nixos-rebuild does but for a remote
MicroVM.

```
nix run .#nixosConfigurations.nixos.config.microvm.deploy.rebuild root@example.com root@my-microvm.example.com switch
```

options.title: microvm.deploy.sshSwitch
options.description:
Instead of restarting a MicroVM for an update, perform it via
SSH.

The host's /nix/store must be mounted, and the built
`config.microvm.declaredRunner` must exist in it. Use
`microvm.deploy.installOnHost` like this:

```
nix run .#nixosConfigurations.nixos.config.microvm.deploy.installOnHost root@example.com
nix run .#nixosConfigurations.nixos.config.microvm.deploy.sshSwitch root@my-microvm.example.com switch
```

options.title: microvm.devices
options.description: PCI/USB devices that are passed from the host to the MicroVM

options.title: microvm.devices.*.bus
options.description: Device is either on the `pci` or the `usb` bus

options.title: microvm.devices.*.path
options.description: Identification of the device on its bus

options.title: microvm.devices.*.qemu.bus
options.description: QEMU bus to which this device is attached (optional)

options.title: microvm.devices.*.qemu.deviceExtraArgs
options.description: Device additional arguments (optional)

options.title: microvm.devices.*.qemu.id
options.description: QEMU device identifier (optional)

options.title: microvm.extraArgsScript
options.description:
A script to provide additional arguments for the hypervisor at runtime.

The script must output a single line with arguments for the hypervisor.

options.title: microvm.firecracker.cpu
options.description: Custom CPU template passed to firecracker.

options.title: microvm.firecracker.driveIoEngine
options.description: Type of IO engine to use for Firecracker drives (disks).

options.title: microvm.firecracker.extraArgs
options.description: Extra arguments to pass to firecracker.

options.title: microvm.firecracker.extraConfig
options.description: Extra config to merge into Firecracker JSON configuration

options.title: microvm.firecracker.package
options.description: The firecracker package to use.

options.title: microvm.forwardPorts
options.description:
When using the SLiRP user networking (default), this option allows to
forward ports to/from the host/guest.

::: {.warning}
If the NixOS firewall on the virtual machine is enabled, you
also have to open the guest ports to enable the traffic
between host and guest.
:::

::: {.note}
Currently QEMU supports only IPv4 forwarding.
:::

options.title: microvm.forwardPorts.*.from
options.description:
Controls the direction in which the ports are mapped:

- <literal>"host"</literal> means traffic from the host ports
is forwarded to the given guest port.

- <literal>"guest"</literal> means traffic from the guest ports
is forwarded to the given host port.

options.title: microvm.forwardPorts.*.guest.address
options.description: The IPv4 address on the guest VLAN.

options.title: microvm.forwardPorts.*.guest.port
options.type: 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)
options.description: The guest port to be mapped.

options.title: microvm.forwardPorts.*.host.address
options.description: The IPv4 address of the host.

options.title: microvm.forwardPorts.*.host.port
options.type: 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)
options.description: The host port to be mapped.

options.title: microvm.forwardPorts.*.proto
options.description: The protocol to forward.

options.title: microvm.graphics.backend
options.description:
QEMU display backend to use when `graphics.enable` is true.

Defaults to `cocoa` on Darwin hosts and `gtk` otherwise.

options.title: microvm.graphics.crosvmPackage
options.description: crosvm package to use when running graphics (for cloud-hypervisor and crosvm)

options.title: microvm.graphics.enable
options.description:
Enable GUI support.

MicroVMs with graphics are intended for the interactive
use-case. They cannot be started through systemd jobs.

The display backend is chosen by `microvm.graphics.backend`.

options.title: microvm.graphics.socket
options.description: Path of vhost-user socket

options.title: microvm.guest.enable
options.description: Whether to enable the microvm.nix guest module at all.

options.title: microvm.hotplugMem
options.description:
Amount of hotplug memory in megabytes.

This describes the maximum amount of memory that can be dynamically added to the VM with virtio-mem.

options.title: microvm.hotpluggedMem
options.description:
Amount of hotplugged memory in megabytes.

This basically describes the amount of hotplug memory the VM starts with.

options.title: microvm.hugepageMem
options.description:
Whether to use hugepages as memory backend.
(Currently only respected if using cloud-hypervisor)

options.title: microvm.hypervisor
options.description:
Which hypervisor to use for this MicroVM

Choose one of: qemu, cloud-hypervisor, firecracker, crosvm, kvmtool, stratovirt, alioth, vfkit

options.title: microvm.initialBalloonMem
options.description: Amount of initial balloon memory in megabytes.

options.title: microvm.initrdPath
options.description: Path to the initrd file in the initrd package

options.title: microvm.interfaces
options.description: Network interfaces

options.title: microvm.interfaces.*.bridge
options.description: Attach network interface to host bridge interface for type = "bridge"

options.title: microvm.interfaces.*.id
options.description: Interface name on the host

options.title: microvm.interfaces.*.mac
options.description: MAC address of the guest's network interface

options.title: microvm.interfaces.*.macvtap.link
options.description: Attach network interface to host interface for type = "macvlan"

options.title: microvm.interfaces.*.macvtap.mode
options.description: The MACVLAN mode to use

options.title: microvm.interfaces.*.tap.vhost
options.description:
Enable vhost-net for TAP interfaces.

When enabled, packet processing is offloaded to the kernel's
vhost-net module instead of QEMU userspace, significantly
improving network throughput (~10 Gbps vs ~1.5 Gbps).

Requires the vhost_net kernel module on the host.

options.title: microvm.interfaces.*.type
options.description: Interface type

options.title: microvm.kernel
options.description: Kernel package to use for MicroVM runners. Better set `boot.kernelPackages` instead.

options.title: microvm.kernelParams
options.description: Includes boot.kernelParams but doesn't end up in toplevel, thereby allowing references to toplevel

options.title: microvm.kvmtool.package
options.description: The kvmtool package to use.

options.title: microvm.machineId
options.description:
UUID for this MicroVM, used for:
- Registration with systemd-machined
- SMBIOS system UUID (QEMU only)
- Guest /etc/machine-id initialization when explicitly set

If null, a deterministic UUIDv5 is generated at runtime from the hostname
for machined registration and SMBIOS UUID.

Format: 8-4-4-4-12 hex digits (standard UUID format).

options.title: microvm.mem
options.description: Amount of RAM in megabytes

options.title: microvm.optimize.enable
options.description:
Enables some optimizations by default to closure size and startup time:
  - defaults documentation to off
  - defaults to using systemd in initrd
  - use systemd-networkd
  - disables systemd-network-wait-online
  - disables NixOS system switching if the host store is not mounted

This takes a few hundred MB off the closure size, including qemu,
allowing for putting MicroVMs inside Docker containers.

options.title: microvm.preStart
options.description: Commands to run before starting the hypervisor

options.title: microvm.prettyProcnames
options.description: Set a recognizable process name right before executing the Hyperisor.

options.title: microvm.qemu.extraArgs
options.description: Extra arguments to pass to qemu.

options.title: microvm.qemu.machine
options.description:
QEMU machine model, eg. `microvm`, or `q35`

Get a full list with `qemu-system-x86_64 -M help`

This has a default declared with `lib.mkDefault` because it
depends on ${pkgs.system}.

options.title: microvm.qemu.machineOpts
options.description: Overwrite the default machine model options.

options.title: microvm.qemu.package
options.description: The QEMU package to use.
options.default:
if config.microvm.cpu == null && config.microvm.vmHostPackages.stdenv.hostPlatform.isLinux then
  # If no CPU is requested and the host is Linux, use qemu with KVM support (hardware-accelerated)
  config.microvm.vmHostPackages.qemu_kvm
else
  # Different CPU architectures like darwin or Non-Linux use the generic qemu package
  config.microvm.vmHostPackages.qemu

options.title: microvm.qemu.pcieRootPorts
options.description:
A list of PCIe root ports that can be used for hot-plugging PCIe devices.
This is particularly useful on the Q35 machine type, which does not support
hot-plugging on the base PCIe root bus (pcie.0). Creating root ports allows
attaching and detaching PCIe devices at runtime and can also be useful for
devices that require their own dedicated PCIe slot with a fixed address, etc.
For additional details see the QEMU PCI Express Guidelines:
<https://gitlab.com/qemu-project/qemu/-/blob/master/docs/pcie.txt>

options.title: microvm.qemu.pcieRootPorts.*.addr
options.description: PCIe address on the parent bus.

options.title: microvm.qemu.pcieRootPorts.*.bus
options.description: The PCIe bus on which the root port will be created.

options.title: microvm.qemu.pcieRootPorts.*.chassis
options.description: The chassis number associated with this PCIe root port.

options.title: microvm.qemu.pcieRootPorts.*.id
options.description: A unique identifier for this PCIe root port.

options.title: microvm.qemu.pcieRootPorts.*.slot
options.description: PCIe slot number.

options.title: microvm.qemu.serialConsole
options.description: Whether to enable the virtual serial console on qemu.

options.title: microvm.registerClosure
options.description:
Whether to enable Register system closure's store paths in Nix db.

While enabled by default, this option may be incompatible with a persistent writable store overlay.
.

options.title: microvm.registerWithMachined
options.description:
Register this MicroVM with systemd-machined on the host, enabling management via machinectl.

When enabled, a registration script is generated in the runner package. The host module will call this
script after the hypervisor starts. The VM is registered with class "vm" using the UUID from `machineId`
(or a deterministic UUID derived from hostname).

Supported machinectl commands:
- `list`, `status`, `show` - VM visibility
- `terminate`, `kill` - stop VM (will auto-restart if Restart=always)

Note: `machinectl reboot` stops the VM but won't auto-restart it because systemd treats it as an
intentional stop. Use `systemctl restart microvm@<name>` for restarts.

options.title: microvm.runner
options.description: Generated Hypervisor runner for this NixOS

options.title: microvm.shares
options.description: Shared directory trees

options.title: microvm.shares.*.cache
options.description: Virtiofs caching policy for the file system, ignored when 9p is used

options.title: microvm.shares.*.mountPoint
options.description: Where to mount the share inside the container

options.title: microvm.shares.*.proto
options.description: Protocol for this share

options.title: microvm.shares.*.readOnly
options.description: Turn off write access

options.title: microvm.shares.*.securityModel
options.description: What security model to use for the shared directory

options.title: microvm.shares.*.socket
options.description: Socket for communication with virtiofs daemon

options.title: microvm.shares.*.source
options.description: Path to shared directory tree

options.title: microvm.shares.*.tag
options.description: Unique virtiofs daemon tag

options.title: microvm.socket
options.description: Hypervisor control socket path

options.title: microvm.storeDisk
options.description: Generated

options.title: microvm.storeDiskErofsFlags
options.description:
Flags to pass to mkfs.erofs

Omit `"-Efragments"` and `"-Ededupe"` to enable multi-threading.

options.title: microvm.storeDiskSquashfsFlags
options.description: Flags to pass to gensquashfs

options.title: microvm.storeDiskType
options.description:
Boot disk file system type: squashfs is smaller, erofs is supposed to be faster.

Defaults to erofs, unless the NixOS hardened profile is detected.

options.title: microvm.storeOnDisk
options.description: Whether to boot with the storeDisk, that is, unless the host's /nix/store is a microvm.share.

options.title: microvm.stratovirt.package
options.description: The stratovirt package to use.

options.title: microvm.systemSymlink
options.description:
Whether to inclcude a symlink of `config.system.build.toplevel` to `share/microvm/system`.
This is required for commands like `microvm -l` to function but removes reference to the uncompressed store content when using a disk image for the nix store.

options.title: microvm.user
options.description: User to switch to when started as root

options.title: microvm.vcpu
options.description: Number of virtual CPU cores

options.title: microvm.vfkit.extraArgs
options.description: Extra arguments to pass to vfkit.

options.title: microvm.vfkit.logLevel
options.description: vfkit log level.

options.title: microvm.vfkit.package
options.description: The vfkit package to use.

options.title: microvm.vfkit.rosetta.enable
options.description:
Enable Rosetta support for running x86_64 binaries in ARM64 Linux VMs.
Only works on Apple Silicon (ARM) Macs.

When enabled, the Rosetta virtiofs share will be automatically mounted
and binfmt will be configured to use Rosetta for x86_64 binaries.

options.title: microvm.vfkit.rosetta.ignoreIfMissing
options.description:
Continue execution even if Rosetta installation fails or is unavailable.
Useful for configurations that should work on both ARM and Intel Macs.

options.title: microvm.vfkit.rosetta.install
options.description:
Automatically install Rosetta if missing.
If false and Rosetta is not installed, vfkit will fail to start.

options.title: microvm.virtiofsd.extraArgs
options.description: Extra command-line switch to pass to virtiofsd.

options.title: microvm.virtiofsd.group
options.description:
The name of the group that will own the Unix domain socket file that virtiofsd creates for communication with the hypervisor.
If null, the socket will have group ownership of the user running the hypervisor.

options.title: microvm.virtiofsd.inodeFileHandles
options.description:
When to use file handles to reference inodes instead of O_PATH file descriptors
(never, prefer, mandatory)

Allows you to overwrite default behavior in case you hit "too
many open files" on eg. ZFS.
<https://gitlab.com/virtio-fs/virtiofsd/-/issues/121>

options.title: microvm.virtiofsd.package
options.description: The virtiofsd package to use.

options.title: microvm.virtiofsd.threadPoolSize
options.description:
The amounts of threads virtiofsd should spawn. This option also takes the special
string `\`nproc\`` which spawns as many threads as the host has cores.

options.title: microvm.vmHostPackages
options.description: If set, overrides the default host package.
options.default: if config.microvm.cpu == null then pkgs else pkgs.buildPackages

options.title: microvm.volumes
options.description: Disk images

options.title: microvm.volumes.*.autoCreate
options.description: Created image on host automatically before start?

options.title: microvm.volumes.*.direct
options.description: Whether to set O_DIRECT on the disk.

options.title: microvm.volumes.*.fsType
options.description: Filesystem for automatic creation and mounting

options.title: microvm.volumes.*.image
options.description: Path to disk image on the host

options.title: microvm.volumes.*.imageType
options.description:
Format of the image (only passed to the hypervisor, does not change format of the image created if `autoCreate` is true).

::: {.note}
Only supported with cloud-hypervisor.
:::

options.title: microvm.volumes.*.label
options.description: Label of the volume, if any. Only applicable if `autoCreate` is true; otherwise labeling of the volume must be done manually

options.title: microvm.volumes.*.mkfsExtraArgs
options.description: Set extra Filesystem creation parameters

options.title: microvm.volumes.*.mountPoint
options.description: If and where to mount the volume inside the container

options.title: microvm.volumes.*.readOnly
options.description: Turn off write access

options.title: microvm.volumes.*.serial
options.description: User-configured serial number for the disk

options.title: microvm.volumes.*.size
options.description: Volume size (in MiB) if created automatically

options.title: microvm.vsock.cid
options.description:
Virtual Machine address;
setting it enables AF_VSOCK

The following are reserved:
- 0: Hypervisor
- 1: Loopback
- 2: Host

options.title: microvm.vsock.ssh.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable SSH server listening on VSOCK for host-to-guest connections.

When enabled, the guest's SSH server will listen on the VSOCK interface, allowing the host to connect without
network configuration. Requires `microvm.vsock.cid` to be set.

From the host, connect using:
- For qemu/crosvm/kvmtool: `ssh vsock/<CID>`
- For cloud-hypervisor: `ssh vsock-mux/<path-to-notify.vsock>`
- Or use: `microvm -s <vmname>`
.

options.title: microvm.writableStoreOverlay
options.description:
Path to the writable /nix/store overlay.

If set to a filesystem path, the initrd will mount /nix/store
as an overlay filesystem consisting of the read-only part as a
host share or from the built storeDisk, and this configuration
option as the writable overlay part. This allows you to build
nix derivations *inside* the VM.

Make sure that the path points to a writable filesystem
(tmpfs, volume, or share).
